meaning: n.过度的疲劳,紧张,张力,应变vt.扭伤,损伤v.拉紧,扯紧,(使)紧张,尽力
examples: The rope broke under the strain.
The wall collapsed under the strain.
I had to strain to hear.
One strain of virus then infects all cells, producing a soup full of virus.
Burma's highly mobile migrant population may already be spreading the drug-resistant strain around the world.
